name: Old site of Lleida - Circular tour from Mollerussa
name_original: Sede vieja de Lleida - Vuelta circular desde Mollerussa
region_intro: Road cycling around Mollerussa is characterized by its varied and picturesque landscapes, shaped significantly by the Canal d'Urgell. The terrain features expansive agricultural heartlands, tranquil farmlands, and orchards, predominantly on paved surfaces. While generally flat, the region also offers routes with rolling hills, providing varying gradients for different skill levels. Natural features like the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond contribute to the scenic appeal of the area. Best road cycling routes around Mollerussa The most popular road cycling route is Old site of Lleida - Circular tour from Mollerussa, a 38.2 miles (61.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the agricultural heartlands surrounding Mollerussa. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Poal and Ivars and Vila-sana Lake Loop, an easy 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route circles the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond, an area known for its biodiversity and birdwatching opportunities. Local road cyclists also love the Arbeca Castle – Bellpuig Castle loop from Mollerussa, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail leading through scenic villages and historical castles, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Road cycling around Mollerussa is defined by the Canal d'Urgell, extensive agricultural lands, and rolling hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation gain. The routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mollerussa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 65 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Mollerussa?
A: The terrain around Mollerussa is predominantly characterized by expansive agricultural heartlands, tranquil farmlands, and orchards, mostly on paved surfaces. While generally flat, the region also features routes with rolling hills, providing varied gradients for different skill levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3668455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal d'Urgell&lt;/a&gt; is a significant feature, shaping many of the local pathways.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mollerussa?
A: Yes, Mollerussa has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939642115/el-poal-and-ivars-and-vila-sana-lake-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Poal and Ivars and Vila-sana Lake Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 40.6 km (25.2-mile) path that circles the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, ideal for nature lovers.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa's routes offer views of diverse natural features. You can cycle around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, Catalonia's largest pond, known for birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along the Urgell channel is another notable natural attraction. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; provides a relaxing environment with a small pond.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the cycling routes in Mollerussa?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.5-meter cascade along the Urgell channel, is a popular point of interest that can be incorporated into cycling tours. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3776964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Second Machine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Mollerussa?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Mollerussa are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942323709/old-site-of-lleida-circular-tour-from-mollerussa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old site of Lleida - Circular tour from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the agricultural heartlands.

Q: Are there routes that explore historical sites or castles?
A: Absolutely. The broader Lleida region, including Mollerussa, is rich in historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arbeca Castle – Bellpuig Castle loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading through scenic villages and historical castles, offering a blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, with mild weather and beautiful landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter offers cooler, crisp rides.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Mollerussa?
A: The road cycling routes in Mollerussa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic agricultural landscapes, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there longer, more challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: While many routes are relatively flat, Mollerussa also offers more challenging options for experienced cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallbona de les Monges – Castillo de Maldà loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, covering 62.4 km with over 530m of elevation gain, provide a more demanding experience with rolling hills.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Mollerussa?
A: Mollerussa and its surrounding villages offer various cafes and restaurants where cyclists can refuel. While specific establishments vary by route, you'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments in the towns and villages you pass through, especially on longer tours.
tips: La Seu Vella, also known as the Old Cathedral of Lleida, is one of the most emblematic monuments of the city. Built between the 13th and 15th centuries, it combines Romanesque and Gothic styles. It is located on the hill known as Turó de la Seu Vella, from where you can enjoy panoramic views of the city and the Segrià region.

If our route passes through the town of Alcoletge, we cannot pass by the Church of San Miguel Arcángel de Alcoletge, an important construction from between 1765 and 1773 in neoclassical style with baroque elements.

Bell-lloc d'Urgell and its municipal term, has been a place of passage since time immemorial. The first human traces located are from the early Iron Age and date back to Roman times.
With regard to the period of Roman domination, there were some inhabited towers and spread over what is currently its terminus. This is shown by the ceramics and other objects that have been found, on the occasion of the leveling of different farmlands.



http://www.bell-lloc.cat/municipi/historia-i-patrimoni
